_note: OATH HOUSE — THE LIVING HOUSE content. Staff may edit this file to update today's rhythm, the soundtrack, object stories and availability. No code changes required.

objects.name: The Coffee Cup
objects.title: To begin slowly
body: Before the house fills, there is the cup. Single origin, stone-ground, roasted three streets away and poured by hand while the light is still low and blue.
We do not rush the morning. The first ritual of the day is patience — to stand at the counter, to wait for the bloom, to begin with both hands warm.

objects.name: The Loaf
objects.title: Broken, not sliced
body: A long ferment, a dark crust, a crumb that smells of the field. Bread is the oldest reason people gather — something made to be torn and passed along the table.
Each loaf is shaped the night before and baked at first light, so the house wakes to the smell of it.

objects.name: A Leaf in the Light
objects.title: People and plants
body: The garden is not decoration. It is a second household — tended, watered, listened to. We grow what we can use, and we let the rest simply be green.
Watch a leaf move in the morning air and you understand the whole philosophy of the house: slow, alive, rooted.

objects.name: The Chair
objects.title: To sit together
body: Hand-built from reclaimed timber, each chair carries the small imperfections of the hands that made it. It asks one thing of you: to stay a while.
The most radical act in a fast city is to sit down across from another person and give them your time. That is what this room is for.

objects.name: The Market Basket
objects.title: What the day brings
body: The menu is written by the morning market, not the other way around. Whatever is ripe, whatever is caught, whatever a neighbour leaves at the door — that becomes lunch.
Cooking this way means no two days taste the same. The house eats with the season.

objects.name: The Bottle
objects.title: Low intervention, high trust
body: Natural wine, made by people we know, opened as the shadows grow long. Nothing added, nothing stripped away — only grapes, time and a little faith.
It is not about the label. It is about the hour: the light turning amber, the room loosening, the evening arriving without being announced.

objects.name: The Ceramic Cup
objects.title: Imperfect on purpose
body: Thrown by a single pair of hands and fired at high heat, no two cups are alike. The thumb-mark of the maker is left where it fell — wabi, the beauty of the unfinished.
To drink from an object made by a person you could name is to remember that everything here was touched before it reached you.
objects.meta: Stoneware · thrown by hand in Luang Prabang

objects.name: The Candle
objects.title: Keeping a small fire
body: When the sun leaves, we do not turn the house bright. We light it. Soy wax, dried botanicals, a slow cedar burn that marks the change from day into night.
A flame asks the eyes to soften and the voice to lower. The room becomes intimate the moment it is lit.

objects.name: The Vinyl
objects.title: Tonight's listening
body: {soundtrack}. A record set down with care and played start to finish the way it was meant to be heard.
In the house, sound is not background. We play sides, not songs — and we ask you to listen the way you would read a letter.
objects.meta: Side A · vinyl only · no skipping

objects.name: The Speaker
objects.title: Why listening matters
body: Hand-built cabinets, vintage drivers, a single point in the room where everything resolves. The system was tuned by ear over many late nights until the music simply felt true.
We built a room for listening because attention has become the rarest thing we have. To give it fully — to a record, to a person — is the whole point of the night.

objects.name: The Cocktail
objects.title: Built slowly
body: Stirred, not hurried. House ferments, foraged bitters, ice cut to order. A drink built with the same patience as the morning coffee, twelve hours later.
The last ritual of the day mirrors the first: both ask you to wait, and both reward you for it.

objects.name: The O
objects.title: Rooted in the Real
body: In a world moving too fast, Oath was made as a place to reconnect — through food, through sound, through movement. The two dots are the gathering: people, breath, presence, the pause between songs.
You found the mark. Most people walk straight past it. Come back at a different hour and the house will show you something else — there is always more here than is announced.
— A note from the founders

objects.name: The Full Moon
objects.title: Under a full moon
body: The garden is open late tonight. On full-moon evenings the house lets the dark in — lanterns instead of lamps, the long table moved outside, a slower set on the turntable.
Some things only happen when the sky agrees. If you are reading this, you are here on one of those nights.
